keyPoints: Balance depth of signals with cognitive load by designing adaptive, multi-signal DNL (confidence, novelty, bias, cognitive load) tailored to task and user expertise.
Differentiate trust calibration, reliance, and decision quality in cross-domain, longitudinal study designs.
Pair confidence signals with interpretable rationales and data provenance cues to improve interpretability and calibration.
Plan per-task and per-output transparency, with optional deeper rationale on demand.
Incorporate safeguards against over-trust and cognitive overload, including explicit verification prompts for high-stakes decisions.
Define domain-general metrics (calibration curves, PPV/NPV, likelihood ratios, decision accuracy) and cross-domain evaluation plan.
Provide practical designer heuristics and user-use protocols focusing on creativity, planning, and factual decision tasks.
